rules: [P1] Retrieve the latest DeviceUseStatement for the target device.
[P1] If the latest relevant procedure exceeds the specified interval, create a new order with the given indication; otherwise return a concise FINISH indicating no action.

[P1] Retrieve all active orders for the target medication, identify the newest by effective date and retain it, discontinue older ones (report API limitation if unsupported), and create a new order only when no active orders exist.
[P1] If a required lab is missing, verify whether the protocol mandates its presence; if not, allow a no‑action conclusion.
[P1] Verify the existence of a specific procedure code before creating a related service request; if absent, create it, otherwise return a concise FINISH message indicating no action is needed.
[P1] Retrieve the newest result for each requested lab before making a decision.
[P1] Return a single FINISH JSON array without additional narrative; never return an empty FINISH payload when the task mandates creation or modification, and include performed action details.
[P1] If no relevant record exists after filtered retrieval, report the absence; otherwise, proceed based on thresholds.
[P1] Filter retrieved observations (or other records) by the required timestamp or time window before any calculations.
[P1] If a required action is unsupported by the API, explicitly report the limitation and skip the unsupported step.
[P1] Compare retrieved values against reference ranges, protocol thresholds, or policy windows; compute statistical measures (mean, median) as needed and document assumptions.
[P1] Issue each FHIR request as a standalone line without surrounding narrative.
[P1] Retrieve all relevant records (orders, observations, procedures) for the target entity before deciding; use the most recent date for time‑based logic.
[P1] Parse the task to identify the target entity, event/procedure, and any required thresholds before taking action.
[P1] Perform all GET requests before any POST, PATCH, or DELETE actions, and verify results with a re‑GET after modifications.
[P1] Base actions on available API endpoints; do not assume missing capabilities without checking the specification.
[P1] Summarize actions taken, indicate remaining gaps, and provide a concise rationale with data sources, time windows, calculations, order IDs, and dates.
[P1] When no abnormal values and no required follow‑up, return a single FINISH JSON with a concise justification.
[P1] When the protocol indicates no further action, explicitly state that no medication or repeat testing is required.
[P1] When a time‑based condition applies, compute the interval only if a date is present; otherwise follow the default safety action.
